sexual: Their lips hovered just apart as one hand slid slowly along the waistline.
The sheets were tangled, and clothing lay scattered across the floor.
They moved in synchrony, slow and intent, eyes locked the entire time.
A soft moan was barely audible over the sound of rustling fabric.
A gasp escaped as fingertips traced a deliberate path down bare skin.

shocking: The man’s face twisted in a scream, but no sound came out.
One shoe lay on the sidewalk, surrounded by red streaks and shattered glass.
Half the wall was painted with something that looked like it shouldn't be there.
The body hung unnaturally, limbs at angles no joints could allow.
A child’s toy was crushed beneath the wheel of a burning car.

self-harm: The notebook was full of scribbled apologies and jagged lines.
She stared at the blade, unmoving, the water in the tub slowly rising.
Her sleeves were always long, even in the height of summer.
He winced as the bandage slipped and revealed the fresh wound beneath.
The mirror was fogged, but red streaks showed faintly on the sink’s edge.

violence: Blood dripped onto the pavement as sirens echoed in the background.
One man held the other down, fists rising and falling in rapid succession.
The wall cracked where the head hit it, and the body crumpled.
He swung the bat with full force as the other stumbled backward.
A crowd gathered, but no one stepped in as the screaming continued.

illegal-activity: The truck’s false bottom held dozens of hidden packages.
He keyed in a stolen password and transferred the funds in seconds.
They climbed through the broken fence after dark, carrying something heavy.
The man passed a small envelope under the table, glancing around nervously.
A white powder was carefully weighed and sealed in plastic.

harassment: The texts came every two minutes, all from unknown numbers.
He laughed at her refusal and leaned in even closer.
They jeered loudly, daring her to say something.
He kept walking behind her, ignoring how often she looked back.
She flinched when his hand brushed hers—again.

hate: The sign in the window said “Not Welcome” above a drawing of a face.
The speaker called for action “against them” and the crowd roared.
He was pushed and spat on as others laughed and filmed.
The chant named a group, over and over, with increasing venom.
Graffiti spelled out words that made her stop walking and turn back.
